Apologies for the confusion. If you're referring to the fully dotted braille character used as a separator, you can include it in the `brailleDigits` array as a separate element. Here's the updated code:

```javascript
function mapToBraille(time) {
  const brailleDigits = ['⠝', '⠷', '⠾', '⠄', '⠑', '⠭', '⠱', '⠚', '⠆', '⠤', '⠀']; // Include fully dotted braille character
  const seconds = Math.floor(time) % 1000; //
